title: The Quran
subtitle: Holy Scripture of Islam
description: The central religious text of Islam, believed to be a revelation from God (Allah) through the Archangel Gabriel to the Prophet Muhammad over approximately 23 years.
summary: The Quran is the literal word of God as revealed to Prophet Muhammad. It covers guidance for personal conduct, legal matters, theological principles, and stories of previous prophets. It emphasizes monotheism (tawhid), justice, compassion, and submission to God's will.

verses.text: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.
verses.translation: In the name of Allah, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.

verses.text: All praise is due to Allah, Lord of the worlds.
The Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.
Sovereign of the Day of Recompense.
It is You we worship and You we ask for help.
Guide us to the straight path.
The path of those upon whom You have bestowed favor, not of those who have evoked [Your] anger or of those who are astray.
Allah! There is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth.
There is no compulsion in religion. The right course has become distinct from the wrong.
Allah does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity. It will have [the consequence of] what [good] it has gained, and it will bear [the consequence of] what [evil] it has earned.
Every soul will taste death, and you will only be given your full compensation on the Day of Resurrection.
Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day are signs for those of understanding.
Who remember Allah while standing or sitting or [lying] on their sides and give thought to the creation of the heavens and the earth, saying, 'Our Lord, You did not create this aimlessly.'
O mankind, fear your Lord, who created you from one soul and created from it its mate and dispersed from both of them many men and women.
O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm in justice, witnesses for Allah, even if it be against yourselves or parents and relatives.

Your Lord has decre that you worship not except Him, and to parents, good treatment. Whether one or both of them reach old age [while] with you, say not to them [so much as], 'uff,' and do not repel them but speak to them a noble word.
Establish prayer at the decline of the sun [from its meridian] until the darkness of the night and [also] the Quran [for] dawn. Indeed, the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed.
Successful indeed are the believers.
Then did you think that We created you uselessly and that to Us you would not be returned?
Do the people think that they will be left to say, 'We believe' and they will not be tried?
And those who strive for Us - We will surely guide them to Our ways. Indeed, Allah is with the doers of good.

O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another. Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you.
The Most Merciful.
Taught the Quran.
Then which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?
Blessed is He in whose hand is dominion, and He is over all things competent.
[He] who created death and life to test you [as to] which of you is best in deed.
It is He who made the earth tame for you - so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision.
By the morning hours.
Your Lord has not taken leave of you, nor has He detested [you].
And the Hereafter is better for you than the present.
And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied.
For indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.
Indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.
So when you have finished [your duties], then stand up [for worship].
And to your Lord direct [your] longing.
Say, 'He is Allah, [who is] One.'
Allah, the Eternal Refuge.
He neither begets nor is born.
Nor is there to Him any equivalent.
